description: LTX-2.3 (distilled fp8) video-to-video occlusion / foreground removal using the Obscura Remova LoRA. Graph: axiomgraph/ComfyUIWorkflow 'LTX 2.3 Obscura_Remova'.
_HOWITRUNS: No-build path: a public ComfyUI base image (image) runs comfy_run.py, which on startup clones node_packs into custom_nodes and downloads models + the LoRA onto the Spark node, then converts ltx_Obscura_Remova.workflow.json (UI graph) to API format via /object_info and renders. Nothing is built or pushed; nothing large touches your computer. Trade-off: ~30 GB is fetched on each COLD start (use --idle-hold to keep a warm node between iterations). --convert-only skips the model download (only node classes are needed for conversion), so it's quick.
_PARAMS: Node ids come from the UI graph and are preserved through conversion; patches apply post-conversion, so paths target API format. Primitive nodes (432/302) use input name 'value' by convention; if a patch warns 'not in graph' or inputs look wrong, pull the converted workflow_api.json (written to ShareSync) and check field names.
_LORASTACK: Obscura is the built-in LoRA on node 427 (the workflow's purpose). Extra Tier-1 LoRAs from --lora are chained AFTER it via lora_chain (anchor 427): 326(UNET) -> 427(Obscura) -> your stack -> CFGGuider. Mostly useful for layering a style/effect on top of the cleanplate; for plain generation+effects use the ltx2_generate preset.

ui.title: LTX-2.3 Cleanplate — occlusion / foreground removal (Obscura Remova)

primary_input.label: Input video
primary_input.tooltip: The clip to clean up. Obscura regenerates occluded/foreground regions from the surrounding video.

ui._RESNOTE: This graph auto-orients output to the SOURCE clip's aspect (nodes 425/397/398), so sizes are short-edge x long-edge, NOT width x height. Landscape source -> long x short; portrait -> short x long. One tier per row (16:9 magnitudes); orientation follows the input.

prompt.default: Remove the wooden fence from the foreground.

ui.label: Prompt
ui.tooltip: Obscura's model card format: "Remove the [object] from the foreground." Card examples: 'Remove the window curtains / wooden planks / green plant from the foreground.' If the model must invent the hidden background, describe it too. (src: huggingface.co/WepeNerd/Obscura_Remova model card)

ui.label: Negative prompt
ui.tooltip: Obscura's card specifies NO negative — removal rarely needs one. This is a generic video-quality negative; trim it if results soften. (src: model card has none documented)

ui.label: Obscura strength
ui.tooltip: Obscura's model card: START ~1.3 and raise for COMPLEX removals; this bundled axiomgraph workflow ships 2.3. Lower if the kept subject distorts. (src: model card recommends ~1.3; graph default 2.3)

ui.label: Frame rate (fps)
ui.tooltip: LTX-2.3 ceiling: 50 fps. Clips <=10s can run up to 50fps (even 4K); 10-20s clips are capped at 25fps + 1080p. For v2v match your source's fps. (src: LTX-2.3 docs)

ui.label: Short edge
ui.tooltip: The SMALLER of the two output dimensions (default 720). This graph auto-orients to your source clip: a landscape clip renders long x short (e.g. 1280x720), a portrait clip short x long (720x1280) -- so you set edge LENGTHS, not which one is width. That's why a '16:9' pick can come back 9:16 if the source is portrait. Use the Resolution dropdown for known-good tiers; max 4K, safest divisible by 32. (src: LTX-2.3 docs; graph nodes 314/301/425/397/398)

ui.label: Long edge
ui.tooltip: The LARGER of the two output dimensions (default 1280; native 9:16 is 1080x1920). Pairs with Short edge: orientation follows the source clip automatically, so this is just the long side's length. 4K (3840) is heavy and only for <=10s clips. (src: LTX-2.3 docs)

ui.label: Frames (length)
ui.tooltip: Frame count; LTX REQUIRES 8*n+1 (..., 81, 121, 161). Duration = frames / fps. LTX-2.3 max ~10s @50fps (4K) or ~20s @25fps (1080p). (src: LTX-2.3 docs)
